# UC-18.1.10 · ACI Endpoint Group (EPG) Health

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Performance, Inventory &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We roll each application slice inside the fabric into one score—how many machines attach, whether policies blocked traffic, and whether subnets stayed wired—so owners see their piece instead of one giant fabric dial.*

---

## Description

Splunk rolls Cisco Application Centric Infrastructure **fvAEPg** Application Endpoint Group Managed Objects into per-tenant service views using Distinguished Names under **uni/tn-{tenant}/ap-{application_profile}/epg-{name}**. Each roll-up stitches **fvRsBd** bindings toward **fvBD**, **fvRsProv** and **fvRsCons** attachments into **vzBrCP** Contracts (distinct from **vzAny** shortcuts), counts **fvCEp** endpoints plus **fvIp** bindings, aligns **actrlPermitStats** and **actrlDropStats** rolls—commonly via **actrlRule** statistics—with **vzFilter** and **vzEntry** clause chains, reads **healthInst** snapshots filtered at **epg** scope, accrues **fvFaultInst** by severity, separates **microSeg** micro-segmented EPGs from ordinary **fvAEPg**, interprets deployment immediacy **immediate** versus **on-demand** (idle-leaf quirks), checks Endpoint-as-Data-Class (**DaC**) label coverage, and yields per-EPG SLO math decoupled from fabric-wide **topology/health** boards or catalogue fault timelines.

## Value

Application teams need **tenant→application profile→EPG** tuples where learned endpoints, contract denials, and health numerics align—without spines drowning the signal. Splunk ties **fvRsBd** integrity, micro-segment posture, and fault-weighted rolls so owners spot silent BD drift or contract saturation before vague latency tickets blame applications.

## Implementation

Quick win: hourly saved search on index=cisco_aci sourcetypes cisco:aci:health plus cisco:aci:endpoint flags fv_dn rows where fault_density_score≥9 or slo_pct<99. Deploy Splunk_TA_cisco-aci (Splunkbase 4022); authenticate collectors via aaaLogin.json and aaaRefresh.json; poll fvAEPg children plus actrl statistics into contract_stats; bind dashboard tokens tok_tenant and tok_epg for owner pivots.

## Evidence

Saved search **`aci_uc18110_epg_slo_risk`** on **`index=cisco_aci`** (**`sourcetype=cisco:aci:health`**, **`cisco:aci:endpoint`**, **`cisco:aci:contract_stats`**) plus Dashboard Studio workbook **`ds_aci_epg_health.json`** wired with **`tok_tenant`**/**`tok_epg`** drill tokens.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Cisco APIC clusters on supported 6.0(x)/6.1(x) trains with HTTPS 443/tcp reachable from Splunk-connected collectors; dedicate read-only automation credentials isolated from GUI admins because overlapping sessions collide with **aaaRefresh.json** cookie rotations.
- Splunk_TA_cisco-aci (Splunkbase 4022) deployed wherever APIC and fabric syslog terminates—normalize mnemonic narratives alongside REST-fed sourcetypes instead of collapsing transports.
- Splunk REST API Modular Input (**rest_ta**, Splunkbase 1546) or hardened Python modular scripts executing **POST /api/aaaLogin.json**, **POST /api/aaaRefresh.json**, then parameterized GET pulls that tag JSON into **index=cisco_aci** (rename per governance) as **cisco:aci:health**, **cisco:aci:endpoint**, **cisco:aci:contract_stats**.
- Roles running these searches must include the operational index plus any HEC indexes receiving gRPC forwarders.
- Optional: Nexus 9300 leaf SSH jump access to run **NX-OS** show vlan id \<vlan\> brief while correlating BD bindings referenced by **fvRsBd**.

### Step 1 — Data sources
Schedule HTTPS collectors—60–300s cadence unless **HTTP 429** throttling forces wider spacing—against **GET /api/node/class/fvAEPg.json?query-target=children&target-subtree-class=fvCEp,fvFaultInst,healthInst** so each **fvAEPg** Distinguished Name inherits child **fvCEp** cardinality, **fvFaultInst** severity accruals, and **healthInst** scores aligned to Cisco crash bands. Layer **GET /api/node/mo/uni/tn-{tenant}/ap-{app}/epg-{epg}.json?query-target=self&rsp-prop-include=health** when operators need scoped rsp-prop-include=health confirmation for incident bridges. Poll **GET /api/node/class/actrlPermitStats.json?target-subtree-class=actrlRule&rsp-subtree-include=stats** together with **actrlDropStats** sibling classes so **actrlRule** statistics join parent Distinguished Name paths that roll up toward **fvAEPg** ancestry. Ingest APIC syslog through Splunk_TA_cisco-aci focusing on mnemonic faults **F0467**, **F1394**, **F1545** for cross-transport corroboration. Publish **NDI** **GET https://\<nd-insights\>/sedgeapi/v1/site_health** responses (per-site widgets; map **site_code** tokens) beside Splunk KPIs so operator drilldowns reconcile API versus dashboard lag. Enable **gRPC** streaming telemetry **epg-health** sensor exports from APIC where budgeted, landing structured events on Heavy Forwarders that forward toward **HEC** destinations with sourcetype discipline matching **cisco:aci:health**/endpoint/contract_stats parsers.

### Step 2 — Field extractions
After fieldsummary passes on fresh JSON samples, codify FIELDALIAS macros so dn, **fvAEPg.dn**, **epg_name**, **app_name**, **tenant_name**, bd_dn (from **fvRsBd** targets), **contract_dn** (from **vzBrCP** attachments via **fvRsProv**/**fvRsCons**), **endpoint_count**, ip_count, **fault_count_critical**, **fault_count_major**, **fault_count_minor**, health_score, **deployment_immediacy** (**immediate** vs **on-demand**), micro_seg_isolation (**microSeg** true/false hints), scope (EPG policy scope metadata), and oper_state converge despite camelCase drift. Map **Inventory** semantics for stable **fvAEPg** anchors and **Performance** overlays for latency-friendly health numerics pulled from **healthInst**/**actrl** stats. Deduplicate collectors by stamping collector_id when multiple modular inputs authenticate to the same APIC VIP.

### Step 3 — Initial validation
Compare Splunk health_score versus the APIC GUI Tenants → Application Profiles → EPGs → Operational tab for identical Distinguished Names within one poll interval. Open **Visore** MO inspector on the same **fvAEPg** row to verify child lists align with Splunk **endpoint_count** deltas. Cross-check **NDI** per-EPG health widgets (tokenized dashboards) against Splunk **slo_pct** outputs for the matching **tenant_name**/**epg_name** tuple. On the attached leaf, run **NX-OS** show vlan id \<vlan\> brief referencing the **fvBD** VLAN carried through **fvRsBd**—VLAN silence should coincide with Splunk oper_state anomalies, not benign **on-demand** idle states. Record screenshot evidence whenever Splunk precedes GUI refresh—often **aaaRefresh** misses.

### Step 4 — SPL building blocks
Anchor all rollups on eval helpers coalescing dn→**fv_dn** lowercase strings, rex extracting **tenant_name**, **app_name**, **epg_name** segments, and stats tiers that compute **slo_pct** as the percentage of healthy five-minute slices meeting health_score≥90 across rolling thirty-day windows. Add **endpoint_count** anomaly detectors comparing ep_peak versus ep_floor per **fv_dn** day windows—sudden growth or shrinkage flags data-exfil or decommission drift. Layer contract-violation velocity as **viol_ratio** approximations using **actrlPermitStats**/**actrlDropStats** mixes per poll. Compute fault_density_score = fault_count_critical×3 + fault_count_major×2 + fault_count_minor for weighted paging. Evaluate BD-binding integrity by joining bd_dn lookups—absent subnets or VLAN mapping faults should surge **fvFaultInst** counters even if health_score stays flat. Rehearse bin, stats, sort, where, head, and table stages whenever Splunk_TA_cisco-aci upgrades shift field casing.

### Step 5 — Comprehensive enrichment patterns
Fuse **NDI** per-EPG health tiles with Splunk tok_tenant/tok_epg dashboard tokens so incident bridges open matching Cisco widgets without manual MO copy/paste. Join nightly **CMDB** extracts mapping tenant→application profile→EPG tuples to application owners—surface pager_duty_service aliases for Sev-2 routing. Correlate ServiceNow **CHG** rows tagged for planned EPG retire/onboard against Splunk timelines so synthetic **slo_pct** dips inherit change metadata. Cross-read BD subnet utilization exports (future tie-in to UC-18.1.13) when **fvRsBd** bridges look healthy yet **endpoint_count** stalls—subnet exhaustion misreads as micro-seg failure. Compare micro-segmentation policy intent (uSeg contracts, **vzEntry** tuples) versus realized **actrl** counters; divergences often trace **vzAny** shortcuts or Preferred Group toggles rather than Splunk ingestion gaps. Annotate hunts referencing MITRE ATT&CK T1078 (valid accounts) when **fvIp** bindings shift without matching **CHG**—pair with authentication teams. Map Splunk ITSI service trees so **fvAEPg** KPIs nest under application services then business services, inheriting episode management policies without duplicating fabrics-wide UC-18.1.1 KPIs.

Operational correlation hygiene matters because stretched fabrics occasionally replicate fvAEPg Distinguished Names across pods with overlapping tenant strings yet divergent forwarding domains—preserve pod identifiers parsed from fv_dn before stats merges so Splunk denominators stay faithful to Cisco Managed Object semantics. Nexus Dashboard Insights renders fresher widgets only when WAN latency stays predictable; compare Splunk _indextime drift histograms whenever Insights dashboards insist greener posture while Splunk fault_density_score climbs—both transports may remain truthful under divergent freshness timers rather than implying Splunk ingestion defects outright.

Capacity planners interpreting endpoint_count contractions must corroborate shrinkage against external workload telemetry such as database sessions or Kubernetes replica controllers—silent downsizing mirrors healthy policy pruning yet Splunk alone cannot distinguish benign capacity returns from stealthy endpoint theft until sibling observability confirms intent.

Duplicate collectors authenticated against identical APIC VIP addresses multiply fvFaultInst tallies until collector_id deduplication stamps arrive—audit modular inputs quarterly alongside rotating svc_aci_poll secrets because duplicated aaaRefresh storms inflate fvFaultInst counts independent of dataplane faults.

Documentation packs bundled with Sev-2 escalation ought to cite fvRsBd VLAN witnesses on NX-OS captures, fvRsProv versus fvRsCons tuples describing vzBrCP posture, vzAny reliance explicitly acknowledged, deployment immediacy immediate versus on-demand selections, Splunk screenshots juxtaposed against APIC Operational tabs for reconciliation transparency, plus CMDB-backed owner attestations referencing DaC label completeness whenever Endpoint-as-Data-Class audits accompany microSeg onboarding waves.

Synthetic migration probes intentionally inflate fvCEp placeholders momentarily—tie rehearsal identifiers stored inside CMDB rehearsal flags before paging anomaly detectors keyed purely on deltas.

Splunk Risk-Based Alerting adapters may stitch fds_latest spikes into ticketing queues referencing pager_duty_service aliases refreshed nightly whenever CMDB bulk loads temporarily remap tenant ownership—reconcile stale lookups via KV Store overlays when nightly extracts slip beyond SLA.



Quick win: Hourly saved search on **index=cisco_aci** sourcetypes **cisco:aci:health**/**cisco:aci:endpoint** with **fds_latest≥6** OR **slo_pct\<99** filtered to **tenant_name** tokens—routes before deep **actrl** triage.

### Step 6 — Operational runbook
Trigger EPG-degraded **Sev-2** paging when **slo_pct** breaches 99% rolling thirty days OR **fault_density_score** crosses weighted thresholds while health_score\<85. Fan-out notifications through application-owner workflows keyed off **CMDB** mapping—attach **MTBF** objective ≥2160 hours between production-impacting EPG regressions excluding approved change windows; **MTTR** target ≤90 minutes from first Splunk breach to restored health_score≥90 with verified **fvCEp** stability. Wire dashboard tokens tok_tenant, tok_ap, tok_epg so NOC desks pivot cleanly. Throttle duplicates when **deployment_immediacy=on-demand** by requiring companion **NX-OS** checks or **NDI** confirmation before escalation. Rotate automation secrets quarterly; stale **aaaLogin** manifests as duplicated MO lines with drifting _indextime. Document **Sev-2** bridges to include **fvRsBd** VLAN evidence, **contract_dn** tuples, **microSeg** posture, and **splunk_vs_gui** reconciliation notes.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:health" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:endpoint" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:contract_stats") earliest=-30d@m
| eval fv_dn=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,fvAEPg_dn,distinguished_name,"")))
| rex field=fv_dn "/uni/tn-(?<tenant_name>[^/]+)/ap-(?<app_name>[^/]+)/epg-(?<epg_name>[^/]+)"
| eval mo_class=lower(trim(coalesce(classId,mo_class,object_type,"")))
| eval health_score=tonumber(coalesce(cur,healthScore,metric_value,health_inst_cur))
| eval endpoint_count=tonumber(coalesce(endpoint_count,ep_count,fvCEp_cnt,fvcep_children,"0"))
| eval fault_count_critical=tonumber(coalesce(fault_count_critical,fault_crit,fault_critical_sev,"0"))
| eval fault_count_major=tonumber(coalesce(fault_count_major,fault_major_sev,"0"))
| eval fault_count_minor=tonumber(coalesce(fault_count_minor,fault_minor_sev,"0"))
| eval fault_density_score=fault_count_critical*3+fault_count_major*2+fault_count_minor
| eval contract_dn=lower(trim(coalesce(contract_dn,vzBrCP_dn,vz_contract_dn)))
| eval drops_e=tonumber(coalesce(dropPktCum,drop_pkts,drops_cum,"0"))
| eval permits_e=tonumber(coalesce(permitPktCum,permit_pkts,permits_cum,"0"))
| eval pkt_tot=drops_e+permits_e
| eval viol_ratio=if(pkt_tot>0,round(drops_e/pkt_tot,6),null())
| bin _time span=5m as ts5
| stats latest(fv_dn) as fv_dn latest(tenant_name) as tenant_name latest(app_name) as app_name latest(epg_name) as epg_name latest(health_score) as hs latest(endpoint_count) as ep_cnt latest(contract_dn) as contract_dn latest(fault_density_score) as fds latest(viol_ratio) as vr by fv_dn ts5
| bin ts5 span=1d as slo_day
| eval healthy_slice=case(isnull(hs),0,hs>=90,1,1=1,0)
| stats sum(healthy_slice) as healthy_slices dc(ts5) as intervals latest(ep_cnt) as ep_latest avg(vr) as viol_avg latest(fds) as fds_now values(contract_dn) as contract_dn_vals max(ep_cnt) as ep_hi min(ep_cnt) as ep_lo avg(hs) as hs_avg by fv_dn slo_day
| stats sum(healthy_slices) as hs_good dc(slo_day) as days_seen avg(ep_latest) as ep_avg avg(viol_avg) as viol_roll_avg max(ep_hi) as ep_peak min(ep_lo) as ep_floor latest(fds_now) as fds_latest values(contract_dn_vals) as contracts_seen latest(tenant_name) as tn latest(app_name) as ap latest(epg_name) as epn latest(hs_avg) as hs_mean by fv_dn
| eval slo_pct=if(days_seen>0,round(100*hs_good/days_seen,3),null())
| eval ep_chg_pct=if(ep_floor>0,round(100*(ep_peak-ep_floor)/ep_floor,2),null())
| eval ep_anomaly=case(ep_chg_pct>40 OR ep_chg_pct<-35,1,1=1,0)
| eval slo_degraded=isnull(slo_pct) OR slo_pct<99 OR ep_anomaly=1 OR fds_latest>=9 OR (fds_latest>=6 AND slo_pct<99.9)
| where slo_degraded=1
| sort - fds_latest slo_pct
| head 150
| table fv_dn tn ap epn slo_pct ep_avg ep_peak ep_floor ep_chg_pct viol_roll_avg fds_latest hs_mean contracts_seen slo_degraded ep_anomaly
```

## Visualization

(1) **EPG health-score** grid (latest **health_score**/**crash_band** per **fv_dn**). (2) **Endpoint-count** timechart (**endpoint_count** per **epg_name**). (3) **Contract-violation** rate panel (**viol_ratio**/**drops_e** velocity). (4) **Fault-density** heatmap (**fault_density_score** × **tenant_name**). (5) **Top-N degraded EPGs** leaderboard (**slo_pct**, **fds_latest**, **contract_dn**). (6) Optional **microSeg** facet tile contrasting **microSeg** versus classic **fvAEPg** rows.

## Known False Positives

Sanctioned application-retirement weekends deliberately detach workloads from fvCEp inventories—Splunk rolls show collapsing endpoint_count even though forwarding stays orderly until fvRsBd subnets free capacity for reuse. Deployment immediacy set to on-demand delays dataplane attachment until conversational flows anchor endpoints—idle VLAN segments temporarily report zero endpoints without outages during quiet nights. vzAny catch-all contracts aggregate deny pacing beneath blanket Distinguished Names—per-EPG actrl counters look artificially placid although lateral conversations still obey wide defaults wired elsewhere in the tenant graph. Tenant-scope YAML bulk-import spikes fvAEPg object cardinality while merges reconcile overlapping namespaces—timeline noise resembles churn instead of lateral workload shifts until namespaces stabilize post-commit. microSeg onboarding bursts replay overlapping MO snapshots—duplicate healthInst deltas briefly mimic instability until collectors deduplicate successive aaaRefresh pulls aligned with Splunk linebreaker tuning. Rotate correlation discipline: reconcile suspected anomalies against ServiceNow CHG narratives plus NDI site_health widgets prior to paging application bridges. Planned subnet reclaim phases temporarily orphan fvIp bindings until DHCP timers expire—Splunk ip_count deltas resemble malicious bursts despite benign housekeeping scripts clearing stale leases.
